items.title: Per the NBA’s transaction wire, a lot of Pistons trades are now official. They’ve acquired John Collins, Taurean Prince and Gary Harris and traded Isaiah Stewart, Caris LeVert and Marcus Sasser.

items.body_excerpt: Per the NBA’s transaction wire, a lot of Pistons trades are now official. They’ve acquired John Collins, Taurean Prince and Gary Harris and traded Isaiah Stewart, Caris LeVert and Marcus Sasser.

items.title: John Collins' three-year contract with the Detroit Pistons is $17M flat per season. Year 1 is fully guaranteed, while Years 2 and 3 are both non-guaranteed with late-June guarantee dates, a league source told @spotrac.com.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt: John Collins' three-year contract with the Detroit Pistons is $17M flat per season. Year 1 is fully guaranteed, while Years 2 and 3 are both non-guaranteed with late-June guarantee dates, a league source told @spotrac.com.

items.title: Factoring in the reported contracts for John Collins, Kevin Huerter & Javonte Green and the Gary Harris/Taurean Prince acquisitions, I've got the Pistons at about $42.8M under the luxury tax.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Factoring in the reported contracts for John Collins, Kevin Huerter & Javonte Green and the Gary Harris/Taurean Prince acquisitions, I've got the Pistons at about $42.8M under the luxury tax.

That's enough room to give Jalen Duren a 25% of the cap max without going into the tax.

items.title: Further evidence that the Pistons acquiring John Collins will be a S&T.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Further evidence that the Pistons acquiring John Collins will be a S&T.

In a S&T, a contract must be for three years, but only the first season has to be fully guaranteed.

Pistons could tie this into the Isaiah Stewart to Memphis trade, or as a part of something bigger. TBD!

items.title: Detroit Pistons salary cap situation after signing John Collins and Kevin Huerter.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Detroit Pistons salary cap situation after signing John Collins and Kevin Huerter.

They have $40 million in luxury tax space to re-sign Jalen Duren, but could create more room.

Collins is getting more than the mid-level exception, which means cap space or sign and trade.
